title: galium commune var. scabridum
intro: Galium commune var. scabridum, also known as rough-leaved bedstraw, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Europe, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a diuretic, making it a valuable remedy for urinary tract infections and kidney stones. Galium commune var. scabridum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
